sourceMovie.title: Spectre
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Spectre works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Casino Royale
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Spectre clicked for you, Casino Royale (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Mission: Impossible
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Spectre clicked for you, Mission: Impossible (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Spectre?
faq.answer: Try Casino Royale, Mission: Impossible, Quantum of Solace—each shares DNA with Spectre while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Spectre?
faq.answer: Spectre (2015) is best described as Action, Adventure, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Spectre part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "James Bond Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
